name: Current Affairs in Indian Economy (2): Manufacturing, FTAs & Investment

sourceRefs.label: IBEF/india-briefing.com - PLI Scheme status (Dec 2025); National Mission on Manufacturing (Budget 2025-26); India-Israel FTA talks; Airtel-Google AI hub investment

researchNotes: Second Economy current-affairs topic (2nd lap). Picked dated milestones (scheme launch budget year, FTA negotiation start date, investment announcement date) over monthly GDP/trade figures, continuing the Day-58 lesson about preferring stable facts. Expansion pass (Batch 14): added semiconductor mission progress (Micron's Sanand ATMP facility, Tata-PSMC Dholera fab, Budget 2026-27 allocation) to round out the manufacturing-current-affairs picture.

reading.intro: Covers India's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme results, the new National Mission on Manufacturing, recent trade negotiations, and a major private AI infrastructure investment.

qa.question: What is the PLI (Production-Linked Incentive) Scheme's total outlay, and what had it achieved as of December 2025?
qa.answer: Total outlay: ₹1,97,000 crore (~USD 22.9 billion). As of December 2025, it had generated investments exceeding ₹2.16 lakh crore, driven incremental production of over ₹20.41 lakh crore, and created over 14.39 lakh jobs.

qa.question: What is the National Mission on Manufacturing (NMM), and what are its targets?
qa.answer: Announced in Budget 2025-26, it aims to raise manufacturing's share of GDP to 25% by 2035, create 143 million jobs, and expand merchandise exports to ₹106.05 lakh crore (about USD 1.20 trillion).

qa.question: What did the India-US interim trade agreement achieve regarding tariffs?
qa.answer: It reduced US tariffs on Indian goods to 18%, with improved access across sectors like textiles, agriculture, technology, and manufacturing.

qa.question: When did India and Israel begin negotiations for a proposed Free Trade Agreement (FTA), and where?
qa.answer: 24 February 2026, in New Delhi — the first round of talks, covering sectors like technology, AI, cybersecurity, manufacturing, agriculture, and services.

qa.question: What major AI infrastructure investment was announced in October 2025, and by whom?
qa.answer: Bharti Airtel and Google established India's first Artificial Intelligence hub, in Visakhapatnam, on 14 October 2025 — with a proposed investment of around ₹1,25,000 crore (~USD 15 billion) over 2026-2030, covering data centres, subsea cable connectivity, and clean-energy-supported digital infrastructure.

qa.question: What semiconductor manufacturing milestone did Micron Technology achieve on 28 February 2026?
qa.answer: The Prime Minister inaugurated Micron's Semiconductor Assembly, Test and Packaging (ATMP) facility in Sanand, Gujarat — the first operational semiconductor facility of the current India Semiconductor Mission cycle.

qa.question: What is the status of the Tata Electronics-PSMC semiconductor fab at Dholera, Gujarat?
qa.answer: Tata Electronics, partnered with Taiwan's Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation (PSMC), is targeting FIRST SILICON at its Dholera fab by late 2026 — targeting 28nm-and-below process nodes (covering automotive chips, industrial microcontrollers, display drivers, and IoT devices, not frontier 2nm chips).

qa.question: What did Budget 2026-27 allocate to the semiconductor mission, and what is the overall investment scale approved so far?
qa.answer: ₹8,000 crore — the largest single-year outlay since the programme launched. As of December 2025, 10 projects worth a total of ₹1.60 lakh crore had been approved across 6 states.
